non_claims: either specific worker was the sole failure
the readiness timeout was too short
required_implementation: capture bounded per-worker stdout and stderr into isolated workspace files
retain worker id, GPU index, host, port, process exit state, and startup command SHA-256
retain readiness poll count and the final sanitized HTTP or URL failure per worker
sanitize and byte-bound stdout and stderr tails before evidence serialization
emit a typed worker-startup diagnostic artifact before raising the terminal error
include the diagnostic artifact in the launcher failure bundle rather than only naming it
preserve zero hidden retries, zero worker replacement, and bounded readiness polling
exclude raw environment values, secrets, authorization payloads, and model content from logs
regenerate the governed launcher and add deterministic generator parity tests
create a new post-merge harness materialization lineage without mutating the 426f57d harness
perform metadata-only input inspection before promoting manifest and launcher identities
require a new short-lived authorization only after the remediated harness is integrated
required_negative_regressions: reject unbounded worker stdout or stderr retention
reject raw environment serialization or secret-bearing command material
reject a startup diagnostic that omits worker identity or GPU assignment
reject a readiness failure without process exit state and polling history
reject a failure bundle that records only diagnostic filenames without embedding evidence
reject hidden worker restart, retry, replacement, or fallback
reject mutation of consumed Attempt 5 evidence
reject reuse of the 426f57d harness as the remediated source authority
reject stale launcher notebook bytes after launcher-source changes
reject active manifest promotion before metadata-only harness inspection
reject reuse of the consumed authorization for a future retry
reject model requests or benchmark trajectories during remediation proof
